stem: The tangent line to \( y=[[p]]x^{2}+([[q]])x+([[r]]) \) at \( x=[[x0]] \) crosses the \( y \)-axis at what value of \( y \)?
The tangent line to \( y=[[p]]x^{2}+([[q]])x+([[r]]) \) at \( x=[[x0]] \) crosses the \( x \)-axis at what value of \( x \)?
distractor_rationales: This reports only the correction term \( f(x_0)/f'(x_0) \), forgetting to subtract it from \( x_0 \).
This adds the correction term instead of subtracting it.
This reports \( f([[x0]]) \), the function value, instead of the tangent's \( x \)-intercept.
This reports the slope \( f'([[x0]]) \) instead of the \( x \)-intercept.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( L(x) \) be the tangent line to \( y=[[p]]x^{2}+([[q]])x+([[r]]) \) at \( x=[[x0]] \). Find \( L([[x1]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates the CURVE \( y=f(x) \) at \( x_1 \) instead of the tangent LINE.
This reports \( f([[x0]]) \) alone, forgetting the linear correction term.
This reports only the correction term, forgetting the base value \( f(x_0) \).
This subtracts the correction term instead of adding it.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The tangent line to \( y=\dfrac{[[k]]}{x} \) at \( x=[[x0]] \) crosses the \( y \)-axis at what value of \( y \)?
solution: Since \( f'(x)=-\dfrac{[[k]]}{x^{2}} \), the tangent at \( x=[[x0]] \) is \( y=\dfrac{[[k]]}{[[x0]]} - \dfrac{[[k]]}{[[x0]]^{2}}(x-[[x0]]) \); its \( y \)-intercept is \( \dfrac{2[[k]]}{[[x0]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports \( f([[x0]]) \), the function value, instead of the tangent's \( y \)-intercept.
This has the right magnitude but the wrong sign.
This drops the factor of 2 that comes from the intercept algebra.
This reports the slope \( f'([[x0]]) \) instead of the \( y \)-intercept.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The tangent line to \( y=[[a]]\sqrt{x} \) at \( x=[[x0]] \) crosses the \( y \)-axis at what value of \( y \)?
distractor_rationales: This reports \( f(x_0) \), the function value, instead of the tangent's \( y \)-intercept.
This has the right magnitude but the wrong sign.
This inverts the correct ratio.
This multiplies by 2 instead of dividing by it.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: For what value of \( x \) is the tangent line to \( y=[[p]]x^{2}+([[q]])x+([[r]]) \) parallel to the line \( y=[[m]]x+[[c]] \)?
solution: The tangent's slope is \( f'(x)=2[[p]]x+[[q]] \). Setting this equal to \( [[m]] \) and solving gives \( x=\dfrac{[[m]]-[[q]]}{2[[p]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor of 2 from the power rule on \( px^{2} \).
This adds \( q \) instead of subtracting it.
This forgets to divide by \( 2p \) entirely.
This inverts the correct ratio.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The tangent line to \( y=[[p]]x^{2}+([[q]])x+([[r]]) \) at \( x=[[x0]] \) intersects the \( x \)-axis and the \( y \)-axis, forming a right triangle with the origin. Find the area of that triangle.
solution: The tangent meets the \( x \)-axis at \( x=[[XI]] \) and the \( y \)-axis at \( y=[[YI]] \); the right triangle with the origin has area \( \tfrac{1}{2}|[[XI]]\cdot[[YI]]| = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to halve the product of the two intercepts.
This averages the two intercepts instead of multiplying them.
This adds the absolute values of the intercepts instead of computing a triangle's area.
This drops the absolute value, which can flip the sign when the intercepts have opposite signs.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
